items.title: $800k for mediation not spent prior to fatal stabbing

children.content: Mediation projects were a key part of the Barkly Regional Deal signed in 2019, but many locals are asking why the process seemed to have halted prior to the stabbing death of a young man amid unrest in Tennant Creek.

gallery.altText: A multi-colour flower memorial resting on the edge of a curb in front of a home, with orange paint.

thumbnail.altText: A multi-colour flower memorial resting on the edge of a curb in front of a home, with orange paint.

items.title: Government responses criticised two months on from Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Eight weeks on from the alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby, Alice Springs community leaders are calling for an audit to determine why federal and territory government spending does not seem to be improving Indigenous living conditions and safety.

image.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

items.title: Deputy coroner releases findings into teen's suicide

children.content: A 17-year-old school captain, who was dux of year 12 and a talented athlete, appeared to have everything ahead of him. Then he took his own life.

image.altText: A graphic of a white silhouette and a white spotlight behind him with a blue background sketch marks in it.

items.title: Two men arrested, denied bail after violent unrest in Central Australia

children.content: Two men have been arrested and denied bail after a violent disturbance in Yuendumu, in which multiple vehicles were allegedly set alight by armed offenders.

image.altText: Gold lettering on the side of a building spells out: Law Courts Alice Springs

items.title: Final beanie festival a hit with crowds as $400k worth of caps sold

children.content: The 30th and final Alice Springs Beanie Festival has been a sellout, setting a new record of $400,000 worth of beanies sold in three days.

image.altText: A woman with short brown bob and red shirt, wearing a pink beanie which features two large black cockatoos on top of it.

items.title: Church youth leader sentenced for sexually assaulting 13-year-old

children.content: A man who assaulted a girl in his car has dodged a lengthy jail term, with a judge saying there were "exceptional circumstances" that warranted a discounted sentence.

image.altText: Joshua Emonson poses for a photo with four other people

items.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on SA coast

children.content: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

image.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: Egg shortage unlikely as poultry industry braces for deadly bird flu

children.content: The poultry industry says it is concerned but prepared as the deadly H5 avian influenza variant reaches Australian shores for the first time.

gallery.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

thumbnail.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

items.title: 'Look at us go!': First Nations actors celebrate Logies nods

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

image.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

items.title: You won't see Hugh Jackman the same after The Death of Robin Hood

children.content: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

image.altText: Hugh Jackman in The Death of Robin Hood

items.title: Can Australia still make the knockout rounds of the World Cup?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is well underway and it's starting to become clear which teams are progressing through to the next round of competition. But, Australia's fate still isn't sealed.
Australia currently sitting second in its group with one win against Türkiye and one loss against the United States.
We break down how the new qualifying process works, what's changed, and what the Socceroos need from their match on Friday against Paraguay.

image.altText: Footballer in yellow jersey on blue background smiles with arms raised. Text: A guide to the World Cup knockout & Australia's ch

gallery.altText: Footballer in yellow jersey on blue background smiles with arms raised. Text: A guide to the World Cup knockout & Australia's ch

thumbnail.altText: Footballer in yellow jersey on blue background smiles with arms raised. Text: A guide to the World Cup knockout & Australia's ch

items.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie is dead, US media reports

children.content: CNN reported that one of the notes claimed that the mother of NBC's Today show host, Savannah Guthrie, was dead, and those who kidnapped her did not mean to kill her, but she died shortly after her disappearance.

image.altText: A photo of Savannah Guthrie alongside her mother Nancy behind a crowd with posters.

items.title: WiseTech executive chair Richard White denies human trafficking reports

children.content: WiseTech initially gained ground but is now in the red again after Monday's 18 per cent plunge. The ASX is seesawing between gains and losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The blurred side profile of a man walking past a screen that says "ASX welcomes WiseTech Global".

items.title: Award-winning artwork revealed as 'blatant copy'

children.content: The Doyles award committee is investigating why it took nearly 12 months to discover a winning landscape painting was an imitation of another artist's work.

image.altText: A photo of an artwork depicting two childlike figures at the beach.

items.title: Security agencies issue blunt 'act swiftly' warning on AI

children.content: The Five Eyes agencies warn leaders must "act swiftly" to address the imminent threat as AI increases the "speed, scale and sophistication of cyber threats".

image.altText: A row of men in suits sitting on a stage

items.title: Perfect dupes of ABC articles are fuelling an industrial-scale scam

children.content: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

gallery.altText: An illustration of a phone inside a bear trap

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of a phone inside a bear trap

items.title: Nose landing gear failure could have caused fatal plane crash, ATSB says

children.content: A plane crash that killed two people and injured several others in Adelaide earlier this year could have been caused by a failure in the plane's nose landing gear, an ongoing investigation has found.

image.altText: Smoke coming from buildings behind a fence

items.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

image.altText: Lionel Messi waves to the crowd as he walks off the field after a game

items.title: High cost of living fuels record regional migration, report says

children.content: The rate at which capital city dwellers are moving the regions is at its highest level on record, which the report's author says shows the trend is more than a post-COVID blip.

gallery.altText: Aerial view of sailing boats on a water with a city as a backdrop

thumbnail.altText: Aerial view of sailing boats on a water with a city as a backdrop

avatar.alt: A profile image of a woman with dark hair.

items.title: 'They've lost the plot': UK Labour could learn from Rudd-Gillard soap opera

children.content: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

image.altText: Ken Rudd, wearing a dark suit, gestures with his right hand while holding a pen. Julia Gillard is in the background.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Photo gives clues to missing man's final hours before disappearance

children.content: A previously unreleased image answers one question in the case of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan, but many others remain.

image.altText: Jack McLennan jumps into Barambah Creek hours before he vanished.

items.title: Quick hits: Wild weather, Messi magic and Merlin meets the president

children.content: Some wild weather brings the World Cup to a standstill, but not before Lionel Messi lights it up to rewrite the record books. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: Haaland, Mbappe and Messi make it another magic World Cup day

children.content: Lionel Messi breaks a World Cup scoring record, Kylian Mbappe and France put the foot down against Iraq after big weather delay, Erling Haaland strikes again for Norway and Jordan is eliminated.

image.altText: Erling Haaland holds his arms skyward in celebration

gallery.altText: Merlin the duck, Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe

thumbnail.altText: Merlin the duck, Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe

items.title: Australians have record-low trust in the United States, new poll finds

children.content: The Lowy Institute's latest poll shows Australia's trust in the US has fallen to a record low.

image.altText: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump walk together along the Temple of Heaven.

items.title: Logging company gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria

children.content: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

image.altText: A semi-trailer loaded with logs in a traffic lane approaching a port. The large Spirit of Tasmania ship is in front of it.

items.title: 'We lost our son': India's medical exams scandal leads to tragedy and uproar

children.content: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.
